---
title: Brewing pour-over coffee
date: 2024-03-21
tags: [cooking, coffee, brewing]
---

Pour-over is the most honest brewing method I know. Anything wrong with the beans, the grind, or the water is on full display in the cup, with no crema or milk to hide behind. I use a conical dripper, a paper filter rinsed with hot water, and a gooseneck kettle.

My default recipe is fifteen grams of coffee to two hundred and fifty grams of water. A thirty-second bloom with double the coffee weight in water releases the carbon dioxide, then I pour in slow concentric circles and aim to finish the draw at three minutes.

Grind size matters more than anything else. Too coarse and the cup is weak and sour; too fine and it chokes the filter and turns bitter. I adjust by a single click of the grinder at a time and take notes on what I tried, which is the only way I have ever made progress.
